reasoning: The programmatic signals identified a critical defect where header/footer elements were retained in the output across all 4 pages, creating pervasive accessibility issues. Additionally, there is a major defect regarding mixed list numbering types, suggesting flattened sub-points. According to the count-based routing rules, the presence of any critical defect mandates a REJECT routing, despite the high LLM fidelity scores.

concerns.description: Header/footer elements retained in output (4): State of North Carolina | Environmental Quality | Water Reso; Page 2 of 4; Page 3 of 4
concerns.analysis: header_footer_count=4, pages_affected=4/4. Header/footer text from extraction JSON is present in the rendered HTML across most pages — pervasive pipeline failure. Screen readers will encounter duplicate artifacts on every page.

concerns.description: Mixed numbering types at same list level (1 list(s)): p1:letter+roman — possible sub-point flattening
concerns.analysis: flat_lists=1. Lists with mixed numbering types (numeric+letter or numeric+roman) at the same depth level suggest sub-points were flattened.

On pages 3 and 4, the source PDF has bolded and underlined terms at the beginning of each bullet point (e.g., 'Environmental Justice', 'Risk Classification', 'Beneficial Reuse'). The output JSON strips all visual emphasis (bold markdown and underline) from these terms, presenting them as plain text.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Environmental Justice – One speaker was from Lee County representing Lee County NAACP...
concerns.output_quote: "text": "Environmental Justice – One speaker was from Lee County representing Lee County NAACP..."
concerns.location: Page 3, first bulleted list under section II; and all bulleted lists under Section III (Pages 3-4)
